Facts.text: Second Council of Lyon (14th ecumenical council) opens, convened by Pope Gregory X
1,200 Jews of Toledo, Spain killed by Count Henry of Trastamara
Portuguese explorer Pêro da Covilhã departs Santarém on a mission to India and Abyssinia - succeeds in reaching both, laying the ground for future Portuguese voyages of exploration
Congress of Cologne forms in the Netherlands
Cornelis Gooyer establishes the first permanent Dutch settlement on Mauritius, then uninhabited
First English translation of the Qur'an "Alcoran of Mahomet" is published in London by Alexander Ross, based on a French translation
Isaac B. Fubine of Savoy in The Hague patents macaroni
Theatre Royal opens in Drury Lane, London
Stockholm's medieval royal castle is destroyed by fire, the Codex Gigas (world's largest extant medieval illuminated manuscript) survives by being thrown out a window
The city of New Orleans is founded by French explorer Jean-Baptiste Le Moyne de Bienville
Jews are expelled from Ukraine by Empress Catherine I of Russia
French troops conquer Maastricht in the War of the Austrian Succession
HMS Victory launched; Admiral Nelson's flagship at Trafalgar, 40 years later
Samuel Hearne explores Copper Mine River of Canada
Turkish state of Bukovina secedes from Austria
Captain Robert Gray is the first European to discover Grays Harbor (Washington state)
Influential Gothic romance "The Mysteries of Udolpho" is published by Ann Ratcliffe in London
Indiana Territory organized
Japanese Emperor Kōkaku abdicates in favour of his son Emperor Ninkō
Greece becomes independent, Otto of Bavaria is chosen as king
The settlement of Mayagüez elevates Puerto Rico to the royal status of villa by the government of Spain
Tornado strikes Natchez, Mississippi, killing 317
American Medical Association founded in Philadelphia
Prussians stop insurrection in Varsovia
Argentina and Brazil sign a navigation pact
Riot occurs between pro-secessionist & Union supporters in Knoxville, Tennessee
Battle of West Point, Virginia  at Eltham's Landing, Barnhamsville
Much of Enschede, Netherlands, destroyed by fire
South Dakota Historical Society is founded as the Old Settlers Association of Dakota Territory; organization renamed after statehood attained in 1890
Battle of Wilderness ends inconclusively in Virginia, Union losses 17,666; Confederates 7,500
Skirmish at Port Walthall Junction, Virginia (Drewry's Bluff)
Blacks stage ride-in to protest segregation in New Orleans
US marines attack Panama
German SS Schiller sinks near Scilly Islands, 312 killed
Cincinnati Enquirer first uses term "bullpen" to indicate baseball field foul territory where late-coming spectators were herded like cattle
John E. W. Thompson named US minister to Haiti
Édouard Lalo's opera "Le roi d'Ys" premieres in Paris
Battle in Bunyoro: British Captain Frederick Lugard stops Muslim rebellion, 300 killed

Russian scientist Alexander Stepanovich Popov demonstrates to the Russian Physical and Chemical Society his invention - the world's first radio receiver in St. Petersburg. Celebrated as Radio Day in Russia.
La Soufrière volcano on St Vincent kills 1,680 people
Flexible Flyer trademark registered
Central American Court of Justice inaugurated in San Jose, Costa Rica - first standing tribunal for International Law in the world
Charles Collier wins 1st Isle of Man TT Race: (38.22 mph)
Emperor Franz-Joseph celebrates his golden jubilee with festivities throughout the Austro-Hungarian empire
Construction begins on first 100 houses in Ahuzat Bayit (Tel Aviv)
An ambassadorial conference in St Petersburg, Russia, awards the town of Silistria to Rumania in compensation for Bulgaria's other territorial gains in the First Balkan War
British House of Commons rejects women's right to vote
Plot by English suffragettes to blow up part of St Paul's cathedral thwarted when the bomb is discovered
US Congress establishes Mother's Day
A draft of the Versailles Treaty is shown to the Germans
USSR recognizes Georgia's independence
Belgian soccer team defeats Netherlands: 1-2
MLB New York Giants Jesse Barnes throws a no-hitter against the Philadelphia Phillies, in 2-0 win
Mine strike at Belgian Borinage railroad
Peruvian Torre forms APRA, Alianza Popular Revolutionaria Americana
1st projection planetarium opens at Deutsche Museum in Munich, Germany
MBA Pittsburgh Pirate shortstop Glenn Wright makes an unassisted triple play
Phillies have their 8th game postponed in a row
Angelos Sikelianos organizes the first Delphic Festival in Delphi to celebrate the ancient Greek Delphic ideal
SF Municipal Airport (Mills Field) dedicated
United Kingdom lowers the voting age for women from 30 to 21
Duleepsinhji scores 333 for Sussex v Northants in 330 mins
Part of Khabarovsk becomes a Jewish Autnomous Region
Pulitzer prize awarded to Sidney Kingsley for his play "Men in White"
World's largest pearl (6.4 kg) found at Palawan, Philippines
Dutch Minister of Justice Goseling calls fugitives of Nazi-Germany "undesired strangers"
British House of Commons begins its Norway Debate on the conduct of the war after Germany invades Norway, leads toWinston Churchillbecoming Prime Minister
Battle of Coral Sea ends stopping Japanese expansion
Nazi decree orders all Jewish pregnant women of Kovno Ghetto executed
British 11th Hussars occupy Tunis
Dutch men 18-35 obliged to report to labor camps
US 1st Armour division occupies Ferryville, Tunisia
US 9th Infantry division occupies Bizerta/Bensert, Tunisia
John Hersey is awarded a Pulitzer Prize for his novel "Bell for Adano"
Princess Irene Brigade moves into The Hague, Netherlands
SS open fire on crowd in Amsterdam, killing 22
World War II: British troops enter Utrecht, Netherlands
Tokyo Telecommunications Engineering (later renamed Sony) is founded with around 20 employees
William H. Hastie is inaugurated as the first Black governor of the U.S. Virgin Islands
"Kraft Television Theater" premieres on NBC
Paraguayian government unleashes contra revolt
Nazi collaborator V-Mann Antonius van de Waals sentenced to death
Roem–Van Roijen Agreement negotiated in Jakarta between Indonesian Republican Mohammad Roem and Jan Herman van Roijen for Netherlands - paves the way for independence
International Olympic committee allows Russia to participate in 1952 Olympics
Pulitzer Prize for Fiction awarded to Conrad Richter, for his novel "The Town"
The concept of the integrated circuit, the basis for all modern computers, is first published by Geoffrey Dummer
Record 537-kg swordfish is caught by L.E. Marron in Chile
French surrender to Vietminh after 55-day siege at Dien Bien Phu
US, Great Britain & France reject Russian membership of NATO
USSR signs peace treaty with France & Great Britain
West European Union established
Battle at Oran, Algeria, kills 300
NY Giant Bill White, homers in his 1st at bat
MLB Cleveland Indians pitcher Herb Score is hit in the face by a line drive by New York Yankees Gil McDougald at Municipal Stadium; Score misses the rest of the season
US Air Force Major Howard Johnson sets world aircraft altitude record in a Lockhead F-104 Starfighter at 27,810 m
Dodgers Larry & Norm Sherry are baseball's 10th brother battery
LA Dodger Norm Sherry's 11th HR wins the game for brother Larry
Pulitzer Prize for General Nonfiction awarded to Theodore H. White  for "The Making of the President 1960"
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Christmas Island
SETC Telstar 2 launched (apogee 6,700 miles (10,800 km))
WAOW TV channel 9 in Wausau, Wisconsin (ABC) begins broadcasting
Mamas & Papas' "Monday Monday" hits #1
NY Yankees fire manager Johnny Keane
Lt General Robert E Cushman, Jr, USMC, becomes deputy director of CIA
Pulitzer Prize for Fiction awarded to Eudora Welty for "Optimist's Daughter"
Pulitzer Prize for Poetry awarded to Robert Lowell  for "Dolphin"
Small Astronomy Satellite Explorer 53 launched to study X-rays
5th UNCTAD-conference opens in Manila
Gary Roenicke hits into Orioles 13th triple play (Oakland)
Paul Geidel Jr, American murderer, and longest-serving prison inmate in the United States, paroled after 68 years, 296 days, at the age of 86.
Samm-Art Williams' "Home" premieres in NYC
Yugoslav President JosipTitois buried
Californian federal jury rules NFL violates antitrust laws in preventing Oakland Raiders move to Los Angeles Coliseum
IBM releases PC-DOS version 1.1
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
August Hoffman performs record 29,051 consecutive sit-ups
Iraq bombs a Tehran oil refiner
Phillies outfielder Garry Maddox, retires
105°F in Sacramento, California
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
Mark Merrony (Wales) cycles for 30 mins in Nepal at 21,030 feet
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Haryana beat Bombay in the Ranji Cricket Trophy final by 2 runs
27th Amendment to US Constitution is ratified; bars increases to congressional pay from taking effect until after an intervening election
5 NYC cops arrested in Hauppauge, Long Island, for selling cocaine
US space shuttle STS-49 launched, the maiden voyage of Endeavour
South Africa agrees to multi-racial elections
Denver Nuggets become NBA's 1st #8 seed to beat a #1 seed (Seattle)
Twins beat Indians 10-9 in 17 innings, 6 hours & 36 minutes
ComedianMartin Lawrencesuffers a nervous breakdown
Expos scores 13 in 6th at Giants
Galileo, 4th Ganymede Flyby (Orbit 8)
Mercedes-Benz buys Chrysler for $US40 billion and forms DaimlerChrysler in the then largest industrial merger in history
Guinea-Bissau President João Bernardo "Nino" Vieira is ousted in a military coup

Jury finds "The Jenny Jones Show" and Warner Bros. liable in death of Scott Amedure, after purposely deceiving Jonathan Schmitz to appear on a same-sex crush episode. Schmitz later kills Amedure, jury awards Amedure's family $25 million
Kosovo War: In Federal Republic of Yugoslavia, three Chinese citizens are killed and 20 wounded when a NATO aircraft bombs the Chinese embassy in Belgrade
China Northern Airlines Flight 6136, operated by an MD-82, plunges into the Yellow Sea, killing all 112 people on board
Arsenal thrash Southampton, 6-1 at Highbury to start Gunners' amazing English Premier League record 49-game unbeaten streak; ends 24 October, 2004 with 2-0 defeat at Manchester United
Ehud Netzer of Hebrew University announces he has discovered the tomb of Herod the Great at Herodium, West Bank
NATO air strike kills 14 and woulds 6 civilians in Afghanistan's Badghis Province
Paeleoclimatological research claims dinosaur flatulence may have warmed the earth
20 people are killed and 36 are injured after a gas tanker explodes in Ecatepec de Morelos, Mexico
4 people are killed after the Mayon Volcano erupts in the Philippines
55 people are killed by a Boko Haram attack in Nigeria
8 people are killed and a dozen are injured after bear attacks in Orissa, India
Delaware becomes the 11th US State to legalize same-sex marriage
Dow Jones Industrial Average and S&P 500 set record highs
Italian cargo ship Jolly Nero crashes into the port of Genoa, killing seven people
Fighting between pro-Russian and Kiev forces continue amid fears internationally of a civil war in Ukraine
"Baahubali 2: The Conclusion" becomes the highest-grossing Indian box office film ever, earning $120 million
Global tourism accounts for 8% of the world's carbon emissions according to a University of Sydney study
More mudslides in Rwanda kill 18 people bringing the year's death toll to 200
Vladimir Putinis sworn in as Russian President for another 6 years
World Snooker Championship, Crucible Theatre, Sheffield: Welshman Mark Williams beats John Higgins of Scotland, 18-16; as promised, does his press conference in the nude
Denver is the first US city to decriminalize psilocybin "magic mushrooms"
Flemish friar William of Rubruck sets off to convert the Mongols to Christianity, a mission ordered by French kingLouis IX- one of the most famous travel accounts in the Medieval world
English siege of Orleans broken byJoan of Arcand the French army
Louis XIVof France inaugurates The Palace of Versailles
William Pennbegins monthly meetings for blacks advocating emancipation
Burmese King Alaungphaya sends his Golden Letter on rolled gold to KingGeorge IIof Great Britain detailing trade proposals
First US Presidential inaugural ball (forGeorge Washingtonin NYC)
Ludwig van Beethoven's 9th (Choral) Symphony, often regarded as his greatest work, premieres at the Theater am Kärntnertor in Vienna, Austria
First printed copies of "Poems" by Emily, Charlotte, and AnneBrontëare received, published under the pseudonyms Acton, Currer, and Ellis Bell (two copies sold)
German ChancellorOtto von Bismarckis seriously wounded in an assassination attempt
Columbia University approves plans to award the Pulitzer Prize in several categories, after establishment byJoseph Pulitzer
In a testament to his pitching ability, future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerBabe Ruthleads Boston Red Sox to a 1-0 victory against Washington Senators legendWalter Johnson
Pulitzer prize awarded toThornton Wilderfor his novel "The Bridge of San Luis Rey"
Princess Julianaof the Netherlands opens Juliana Canal between Maastricht and Maasbracht
Glenn Millerrecords "Chattanooga Choo Choo" for RCA, it becomes 1st record to be designated "gold"
Liberty ShipGeorge Washington Carver, named after scientist, launched
German assault onTito's hideout in Drvar, Bosnia
Branch Rickeyannounces formation of the US Negro Baseball League
General MacArthurapproves Japanese constitution
Pulitzer Prize for Drama awarded to Frances Goodrich and Albert Hackett for their play "The Diary ofAnne Frank"
"Roy CampanellaNight" Largest baseball crowd at 93,103 at the LA Coliseum see Yankees beat Dodgers 6-2 in exhibition game
Leonid Brezhnevreplaces Kliment Voroshilov as President of USSR
Michael Tal beatsMikhail Botvinnik12½-8½ for world chess championship
USSR announcesFrancis Gary Powersconfesses to being a CIA spy
US PresidentGerald Forddeclares an end to "Vietnam Era"
Diane Chambers' (Shelley Long) final episode on TV comedy "Cheers"
Panamanian voters reject dictatorManuel Noriega's bid for presidency
Champion Puerto Rican jockeyÁngel Cordero Jr.retires after winning 7,057 thoroughbred horse races
Jacques Chiracwins French presidential election
Science fiction film "The Fifth Element" written and directed by Luc Besson, starringBruce WillisandMilla Jovovichis released. Then the most expensive European film ever made.
PopeJohn Paul IItravels to Romania becoming the first pope to visit a predominantly Eastern Orthodox country since the Great Schism in 1054
On the final day of the Premier League season,Thierry Henryscores a hat-trick against Wigan Athletic in the last match played at Highbury
Dmitry Medvedevis sworn in as the 3rd President of the Russian Federation
"Pirates of the Caribbean: On Stranger Tides", directed by Rob Marshall, starringJohnny DeppandPenélope Cruz, premieres in - opening weekend makes $350.6m
Vladimir Putinsworn in for third six-year term as President of Russia
British General Elections: Conservative Party win outright majority andDavid Cameronreturns as Prime Minister
Emmanuel Macronwins France's presidential election, defeatingMarine Le Pen
MTV becomes the first major awards show to adopt gender-neutral categories, withEmma Watsonwinning Best Film Actor andMillie Bobby Brownwinning Best TV Actor
Iran-Contra figureOliver Northannounced as the new President of the US National Rifle Association
Met Gala, "Heavenly Bodies: Fashion and the Catholic Imagination" is hosted byAnna Wintour, Amal Clooney, Donatella Versace andRihannawearing a Pope-inspired outfit
Dome of the Hagia Sophia in Constantinople collapses,Justinian Iimmediately orders it rebuilt
Swedish chemistAlfred Nobelpatents dynamite in England, the first of three patents he receives for the explosive material
RMS Lusitania is sunk by a German submarine off the southern coast of Ireland, with 1,198 lives lost
German Third Reich GeneralAlfred Jodlsigns documents of surrender to the Allies at Reims, France; Soviet Union refuses to recognize it
$180m out-of-court settlement reached in Agent Orange suit
Edvard Munch's painting "The Scream" is recovered 3 months after it was stolen

Louis, Prince of Condé (1530-69), born in Vendôme (d. 1569)

Józef Antoni Poniatowski, Polish general and marshal of France, born in Vienna, Austria (d. 1813)
Princess Frederica Charlotte of Prussia, Duchess of York and Albany, born in Charlottenburg, Germany (d. 1820)
Giuseppe Farinelli, Italian composer, born in Este, Veneto, Republic of Venice (d. 1836)
William Bainbridge, American naval officer, born in Princeton, New Jersey (d. 1833)

Gerhard Moritz Roentgen, Dutch industrialist (founder of Fijenoord dockyard), born in Esen, East Frisia (d. 1852)
Johan Peter Cronhamm, Swedish composer, born in Östra Karup, Skåne, Sweden (d. 1875)
Varina Howell Davis, First Lady of the Confederate States (1862-65), born in Natchez, Mississippi (d. 1906)
Francis Engle Patterson, American Brigadier General (Union Army), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1862)
Carl Neumann, German mathematician and physicist (Neumann-functions), born in Königsberg, Prussia (d. 1925)

Willie Fernie, Scottish golfer (British Open 1883), born in St. Andrews, Fife, Scotland (d. 1924)
William A. MacCorkle, American politician, 90th Governor of West Virginia, born in Lexington, Virginia (d. 1930)
Philippine "Pine" Belder [Mary de Klerk], Flemish actress (Hope of Blessing), born in Amsterdam (d. 1961)
Stanisław Przybyszewski, Polish writer and dramatist (Homo Sapiens, Zur Psychologie des Individuums), born in Łojewo, Prussia (d. 1927)

Harry McShane, Scottish socialist known as "The Last of the Red Clydesiders", born in Glasgow, Scotland (d. 1988)
Frank J. Selke, Canadian Hockey HOF executive (9 x Stanley Cup Toronto Maple Leafs, Montreal Canadiens), born in Berlin, Ontario (d. 1985)
Pavel Sergeevich Aleksandrov, Russian mathematician (topology), born in Moscow, Russia (d. 1982)

Ralph Truman, British actor (Henry V, Treasure Island, Web of Evidence), born in London, England (d. 1977)

Marcel Poot, Belgian baron and composer, born in Vilvoorde, Belgium (d. 1988)
